headline: Politicial scientist Alexandra de Hoop Scheffer: 'Washington is pursuing a strategy of strategic saturation'

This 'burden shifting' challenges Europe to coordinate the organization of its defense industries.

headline: 250 years of American independence: How the text that founded the US – and changed the world – was drafted
subtitle: 'America 250' (1/13). The 13 colonies had been fighting the British Crown for a year when, in July 1776, lawyer Thomas Jefferson completed the drafting of the Declaration of Independence. Adopted by Congress on July 4 in Philadelphia, this document, which established the principle of equality for all – even though slavery was still legal everywhere – founded the United States of America.

headline: 'We sacrificed everything': In Nablus, the relentless decline of Palestinian society
subtitle: In the occupied West Bank, Israel's financial stranglehold on the Palestinian Authority, combined with the proliferation of military checkpoints, has pushed society into unprecedented poverty. In Nablus, the economic heart of the occupied territory, all sectors have faced a sharp decline in living standards.
byline: Lucien Lung and  Marie Jo Sader
